# Regmap Conformance Axil — Register Map

<!-- Auto-generated by IPCraft from regmap_conformance_axil.ip.yml -->
<!-- managed: false — add notes below freely; this file will not be overwritten -->

Hardware register-conformance test IP, AXI4-Lite variant (see docs/hardware-conformance-test-plan.md in this repo, Variant B). Same register map and STIMULUS loopback design as regmap_conformance_avmm (Avalon-MM), proving the AXI4-Lite wrapper on real hardware via a JTAG-to-Avalon-MM master and Platform Designer's Avalon<->AXI bridging -- no HPS/Nios II required.


**Bus interface:** AXIL
**Data width:** 32 bits
**Address width:** 7 bits

---

## Register Summary

| Offset | Name | Access | Description |
|--------|------|--------|-------------|
| `0x00` | `ID` | `read-only` | Fixed magic/version constant -- RO constant readback self-test |
| `0x04` | `SCRATCH` | `read-write` | Plain RW round-trip register, also used for the byte-strobe test |
| `0x08` | `STIMULUS` | `read-write` | Software-writable loopback control. The hand-written core wires these bits into the register file's hardware-side inputs, making every hardware-dependent access type triggerable from the bus.
 |
| `0x0C` | `STATUS` | `read-only` | RO status sourced live from STIMULUS.STATUS_VAL via the core |
| `0x10` | `INT_STATUS` | `read-write` | Multi-bit sticky interrupt flags. HW pulse (from STIMULUS trigger bits) sets a bit; SW clears by writing 1. HW-set beats a simultaneous SW-clear on the ground-truth register file.
 |
| `0x14` | `IRQ_LEGACY` | `write-only` | Plain (non-readable) W1C -- reads back 0 regardless of state |
| `0x18` | `COMMAND` | `write-self-clearing` | SW sets, HW clears (self-clear beats a lingering SW set); reads 0 |
| `0x1C` | `BUSY` | `read-write-self-clearing` | As COMMAND, but readable while set |
| `0x20` | `DIAG` | `write-only` | Write-only diagnostic scratch -- reads 0, but the value reaches hardware |
| `0x24` | `WO_MIRROR` | `read-only` | Readable echo of DIAG.SCRATCH -- confirms the WO value landed in hardware |
| `0x28` | `LINK` | `read-write` | Mixed register -- RO live field plus a change-of-state W1C flag |
| `0x2C` | `CONTROL` | `read-write` | RW register with an enumerated field and a non-zero reset value |
| `0x30` | `CHANNEL_0_CONFIG` | `read-write` | Per-channel RW configuration |
| `0x34` | `CHANNEL_0_COUNT` | `read-only` | Per-channel RO constant, distinct per index -- proves no aliasing |
| `0x40` | `CHANNEL_1_CONFIG` | `read-write` | Per-channel RW configuration |
| `0x44` | `CHANNEL_1_COUNT` | `read-only` | Per-channel RO constant, distinct per index -- proves no aliasing |

---

## `ID` — Offset `0x00`

Fixed magic/version constant -- RO constant readback self-test

| Bits | Field | Access | Reset | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[31:0]` | `MAGIC` | `read-only` | `0xC0FFEE01` | — |

## `SCRATCH` — Offset `0x04`

Plain RW round-trip register, also used for the byte-strobe test

| Bits | Field | Access | Reset | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[31:0]` | `VALUE` | `read-write` | `0x0` | — |

## `STIMULUS` — Offset `0x08`

Software-writable loopback control. The hand-written core wires these bits into the register file's hardware-side inputs, making every hardware-dependent access type triggerable from the bus.


| Bits | Field | Access | Reset | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[3:0]` | `STATUS_VAL` | `read-write` | `0x0` | Passed straight through to STATUS.VALUE |
| `[4:4]` | `SAMPLE_EVT_TRIG` | `read-write` | `0x0` | Rising edge pulses INT_STATUS.SAMPLE_EVT (HW set) |
| `[5:5]` | `ERROR_EVT_TRIG` | `read-write` | `0x0` | Rising edge pulses INT_STATUS.ERROR_EVT (HW set) |
| `[6:6]` | `LEGACY_TRIG` | `read-write` | `0x0` | Rising edge pulses IRQ_LEGACY.FLAG (HW set) |
| `[7:7]` | `CMD_DONE_TRIG` | `read-write` | `0x0` | Rising edge auto-clears COMMAND.START (HW self-clear) |
| `[8:8]` | `BUSY_DONE_TRIG` | `read-write` | `0x0` | Rising edge auto-clears BUSY.ACTIVE (HW self-clear) |
| `[12:9]` | `LINK_SPEED` | `read-write` | `0x0` | Passed straight through to LINK.SPEED (drives change-of-state) |

## `STATUS` — Offset `0x0C`

RO status sourced live from STIMULUS.STATUS_VAL via the core

| Bits | Field | Access | Reset | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[3:0]` | `VALUE` | `read-only` | `0x0` | — |

## `INT_STATUS` — Offset `0x10`

Multi-bit sticky interrupt flags. HW pulse (from STIMULUS trigger bits) sets a bit; SW clears by writing 1. HW-set beats a simultaneous SW-clear on the ground-truth register file.


| Bits | Field | Access | Reset | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[0:0]` | `SAMPLE_EVT` | `read-write-1-to-clear` | `0x0` | Set by a rising edge on STIMULUS.SAMPLE_EVT_TRIG |
| `[1:1]` | `ERROR_EVT` | `read-write-1-to-clear` | `0x0` | Set by a rising edge on STIMULUS.ERROR_EVT_TRIG |

## `IRQ_LEGACY` — Offset `0x14`

Plain (non-readable) W1C -- reads back 0 regardless of state

| Bits | Field | Access | Reset | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[0:0]` | `FLAG` | `write-1-to-clear` | `0x0` | Set by a rising edge on STIMULUS.LEGACY_TRIG |

## `COMMAND` — Offset `0x18`

SW sets, HW clears (self-clear beats a lingering SW set); reads 0

| Bits | Field | Access | Reset | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[0:0]` | `START` | `write-self-clearing` | `0x0` | Cleared by a rising edge on STIMULUS.CMD_DONE_TRIG |

## `BUSY` — Offset `0x1C`

As COMMAND, but readable while set

| Bits | Field | Access | Reset | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[0:0]` | `ACTIVE` | `read-write-self-clearing` | `0x0` | Cleared by a rising edge on STIMULUS.BUSY_DONE_TRIG |

## `DIAG` — Offset `0x20`

Write-only diagnostic scratch -- reads 0, but the value reaches hardware

| Bits | Field | Access | Reset | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[7:0]` | `SCRATCH` | `write-only` | `0x0` | — |

## `WO_MIRROR` — Offset `0x24`

Readable echo of DIAG.SCRATCH -- confirms the WO value landed in hardware

| Bits | Field | Access | Reset | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[7:0]` | `SCRATCH` | `read-only` | `0x0` | — |

## `LINK` — Offset `0x28`

Mixed register -- RO live field plus a change-of-state W1C flag

| Bits | Field | Access | Reset | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[3:0]` | `SPEED` | `read-only` | `0x0` | Sourced live from STIMULUS.LINK_SPEED via the core |
| `[8:8]` | `SPEED_CHANGED` | `read-write-1-to-clear` | `0x0` | Set whenever SPEED changes value; write 1 to clear; no event at reset |

## `CONTROL` — Offset `0x2C`

RW register with an enumerated field and a non-zero reset value

| Bits | Field | Access | Reset | Description |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[1:0]` | `MODE` | `read-write` | `0x1` | — |

Per-channel RW configuration

|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[7:0]` | `VALUE` | `read-write` | `0x0` | — |

Per-channel RO constant, distinct per index -- proves no aliasing

|------|-------|--------|-------|-------------|
| `[7:0]` | `SAMPLES` | `read-only` | `0x0` | — |
